description: Pip-installable multi-agent project management and token optimisation package

tasks.title: Package scaffold
tasks.description: Set up the installable package: pyproject.toml with entry points, top-level agentflow/__init__.py, and empty __init__.py files for every subpackage. CLI entry point agentflow maps to agentflow.cli:main. No logic yet — structure only.

unit: agentflow CLI entry point is importable
all subpackages importable

tasks.acceptance_criteria: pip install -e . succeeds and agentflow --help prints usage

tasks.title: Config system
tasks.description: Layered config loader resolving env vars → .agentflow/config.yaml → ~/.agentflow/config.yaml → defaults.yaml. Pydantic schema validates all fields. Expose load_config(cwd) returning a typed Config object. Ship defaults.yaml with sensible values per architecture.md#config-schema.

unit: env var overrides project config
project config overrides user config
user config overrides defaults
invalid config raises ValidationError with field name
missing config files fall through to defaults cleanly

security_constraints: never log config values that may contain API keys
tasks.acceptance_criteria: all unit tests green; load_config returns typed object with correct precedence

tasks.title: Telemetry — structured logger and metrics
tasks.description: Structured JSON logger emitting newline-delimited records to .agentflow/telemetry.jsonl. Every record includes trace_id, span, task_id, model, tokens_in, tokens_out, duration_ms, status, metadata per architecture.md#telemetry-schema. Expose get_logger(trace_id) and emit_metric(span, **kwargs). OTel exporter hook is a no-op stub for now.

unit: emit_metric writes valid JSON line to telemetry.jsonl
trace_id propagates across nested calls
missing optional fields default to null not absent
file rotation does not lose records

security_constraints: telemetry must not capture prompt content — tokens counts only
tasks.acceptance_criteria: emit_metric produces OTel-compatible JSONL; no prompt content in output

tasks.title: Prompts — oracle, worker, reviewer
tasks.description: Write all prompt files under agentflow/prompts/. Oracle: system.md (senior PE persona), checklist.md (NFR question bank mapped to architecture.md#oracle-checklist), generation.md (artifact output format). Worker: system.md, context_bundle.md. Reviewer: code_review.md, security_review.md. Each file under its soft target of 80 lines.

unit: all prompt files are valid UTF-8
no prompt file exceeds 150-line hard ceiling
oracle system.md contains the NFR checklist section headers
security_review.md references OWASP Top 10
testing_guide.md contains TDD, behavior-not-implementation, and IO mock sections
test_review.md contains scenario coverage and mock appropriateness criteria

security_constraints: prompts must not include example secrets or credentials
tasks.acceptance_criteria: all files exist, pass size check, pass content assertions

tasks.title: Git tools
tasks.description: Worktree lifecycle: create_worktree(base_path, task_id, branch_name), delete_worktree(path), commit_files(worktree_path, message, files). All operations idempotent. Raise typed exceptions on conflict, missing repo, dirty state. Do not shell out with string interpolation — use subprocess with list args.

unit: create_worktree creates branch and directory
create_worktree is idempotent if worktree already exists
delete_worktree removes directory and prunes ref
commit_files stages only listed files
non-list subprocess args raise TypeError
integration: full worktree create → commit → delete cycle on a real temp repo

security_constraints: validate branch names against allowlist pattern before use
tasks.acceptance_criteria: integration test passes on real git repo; no shell=True in source

tasks.title: GitHub tools
tasks.description: PR creation, inline comment posting, and status check reading via GitHub REST API. create_pr(repo, branch, title, body) → pr_number. post_review_comments(repo, pr_number, comments: list[ReviewComment]). get_check_status(repo, pr_number) → CheckResult. Use httpx, not requests. Auth via GITHUB_TOKEN env var.

unit: create_pr posts to correct endpoint with correct payload
post_review_comments batches comments in one API call
missing GITHUB_TOKEN raises AuthError not KeyError
4xx responses raise GitHubAPIError with status code
rate limit response triggers retry with backoff

security_constraints: GITHUB_TOKEN read from env only, never from config files
validate repo name format before constructing URL
tasks.acceptance_criteria: all unit tests green with mocked httpx; AuthError on missing token

tasks.title: Test runner tool
tasks.description: Run the project test suite inside a given worktree directory and return a structured result. run_tests(worktree_path, config) → TestResult(passed, failed, coverage_pct, output). Detect test framework from config (pytest default). Parse coverage from pytest-cov output. Enforce coverage_threshold from config. Return structured result; do not raise on test failure.

unit: run_tests returns TestResult with correct pass/fail counts from parsed output
coverage below threshold sets TestResult.coverage_ok = False
file_validator flags files over ceiling with file path and line count
file_validator returns clean result for files under soft target
run_tests timeout returns TestResult with status=timeout
integration: run_tests against a real minimal pytest project in a temp dir

security_constraints: subprocess in worktree_path only; validate path is within project root
tasks.acceptance_criteria: integration test passes; coverage_ok reflects threshold comparison

tasks.title: Orchestrator — DAG and persistent state
tasks.description: DAG builder from tasks.json: validate no ownership conflicts, build dependency graph, expose topological_order() and ready_tasks(completed_set). Persistent state store reading/writing .agentflow/state.json: task status transitions with timestamps, token totals per task. Expose load_state(cwd) and save_state(cwd, state).

unit: DAG rejects tasks.json with shared owned files
topological_order respects depends_on edges
cyclic dependency raises CyclicDependencyError
ready_tasks returns only tasks whose dependencies are in completed_set
state transition from PENDING to SPAWNED writes timestamp
invalid state transition raises InvalidTransitionError
load_state returns empty state when file absent

security_constraints: state.json must not contain prompt content or API keys
tasks.acceptance_criteria: all unit tests green; ownership conflict caught before any worker spawns

tasks.title: Contract and test skeleton generator
tasks.description: Given a task schema entry, generate: (1) stub Python files with function signatures and docstrings but NotImplementedError bodies, (2) test skeleton files with test class, method names from test_requirements.unit, and IO mock fixtures. (3) mock files for declared IO boundaries. Commit stubs to main before workers spawn. Output is deterministic given the same task input.

unit: stub file contains all function signatures from task owns list
stub function bodies raise NotImplementedError
test skeleton contains one test method per unit scenario
test skeleton imports the stub module correctly
mock fixtures are generated for each IO boundary declared
output is idempotent on second call with same task

tasks.acceptance_criteria: generated stubs are valid Python; test skeleton is importable

tasks.title: Worker context builder
tasks.description: Given a task schema entry and the project's architecture.md, assemble the minimal context bundle and write it to .agentflow/context/<task-id>.md. The worker runner reads this file as its opening prompt — no in-memory coupling. Include: task brief, owned files, read-only files, contract stub contents, the relevant architecture.md section (by anchor), .agentflow/test_strategy.md (always included read-only), security constraints, test scenarios, and config snapshot. Exclude everything else. Measure token count of bundle and emit as metric.

unit: bundle contains task description and acceptance criteria
bundle contains owned file list
bundle excludes files not in owns or reads
bundle includes only the anchored section of architecture.md
bundle includes .agentflow/test_strategy.md as read-only section
token count metric is emitted after build
bundle under 200k characters (hard guard)

security_constraints: bundle must not include GITHUB_TOKEN or API keys from env
tasks.acceptance_criteria: bundle contains exactly the specified sections; token metric emitted

tasks.title: Headless worker agent runner
tasks.description: Run a headless Anthropic API agent for a single task. Read .agentflow/context/<task-id>.md and use its contents as the opening user message. Agent loop: call API → parse tool calls (read file, write file, run tests) → continue until tests green and coverage met → call create_pr. Emit token spans per API call via token_tracker. On context budget exhaustion: compress progress and restart with fresh session. Max 2 restarts before escalating to orchestrator.

unit: run returns WorkerResult with pr_number on success
run returns WorkerResult with status=escalate after max restarts
token spend per API call is emitted to telemetry
tool call for write_file is sandboxed to owned files only
test failure triggers continued loop, not immediate exit

security_constraints: worker may only write to files in its owns list
worker may not execute arbitrary shell commands
tasks.acceptance_criteria: WorkerResult returned in all paths; file write sandbox enforced

tasks.title: Code reviewer agent
tasks.description: Headless reviewer that reads a PR diff and posts inline comments. Context: PR diff + contract stubs + relevant architecture section. Reviews for: contract adherence (does implementation match stub interfaces?), architecture conformance (are file size limits respected? correct module boundaries?), obvious logic errors. Posts comments via github.post_review_comments. Returns ReviewResult(issues_count, severity_distribution).

unit: review returns ReviewResult with correct issue count
comments are posted as inline review comments not PR body
file exceeding size ceiling is flagged as HIGH severity
contract mismatch is flagged with specific function name
empty diff returns ReviewResult with zero issues

tasks.acceptance_criteria: ReviewResult returned; inline comments posted to correct line numbers

tasks.title: Security reviewer agent
tasks.description: Headless security reviewer. Context: PR diff + security constraints from task schema + compliance requirements from oracle output. Checks: OWASP Top 10 patterns, hardcoded secrets, SQL injection vectors, missing input validation at system boundaries, compliance constraint violations. Posts inline comments via github tools. Returns SecurityReviewResult(critical_count, high_count, findings).

unit: hardcoded secret pattern triggers CRITICAL finding
shell=True usage triggers HIGH finding
missing input validation at API boundary triggers HIGH
clean diff returns SecurityReviewResult with zero criticals
compliance constraint violation references the constraint string

security_constraints: reviewer output must not echo secret values — reference location only
tasks.acceptance_criteria: CRITICAL findings block PR merge; all findings reference file and line

tasks.title: Token tracker
tasks.description: Per-span token attribution integrated with agentflow_ledger.json. track_span(task_id, span_name, tokens_in, tokens_out) appends to ledger. Expose session_total(task_id) and project_total(). Enforce per_worker token budget from config: emit warning at 80%, return BudgetExceeded signal at 100% for orchestrator to act on. Shadow model: accumulate what single-session total would have been.

unit: track_span appends record to ledger file
session_total sums tokens_in + tokens_out for task_id
warning emitted at 80% of per_worker budget
BudgetExceeded returned at 100% not raised
shadow_total accumulates correctly across task restarts
project_total aggregates all task totals

security_constraints: ledger must not contain prompt text — token counts only
tasks.acceptance_criteria: BudgetExceeded signal returned at threshold; shadow ratio calculable

tasks.title: Orchestrator — project manager and merge sequencer
tasks.description: Top-level orchestrator: reads tasks.json + state.json, runs the PM loop. Spawns workers for all READY tasks up to parallelism limit from config. Handles WorkerResult: success→PR_OPEN, escalate→human notification. Triggers reviewer pipeline after PR_OPEN. Transitions state on reviewer completion. Merge sequencer: after HUMAN_APPROVED, merges worktree branches into main in topological order, deletes worktrees.

unit: ready tasks are spawned up to parallelism limit
task with unmet dependency is not spawned
WorkerResult escalate triggers human notification and halts task
CRITICAL security finding blocks HUMAN_APPROVED transition
merge sequencer processes tasks in topological order
failed merge rolls back and surfaces error without corrupting state

tasks.acceptance_criteria: full lifecycle runs from PENDING to MERGED in integration scenario; state.json accurate throughout

tasks.title: Design Oracle
tasks.description: Interactive CLI conversation loop with senior PE persona. Loads system.md prompt. Maintains message history. After each exchange evaluates checklist.py confidence score against architecture.md#oracle-checklist items. When all items resolve, proposes generation to user (Option B). On confirmation calls artifact_generator to produce architecture.md + tasks.json + .agentflow/design_session.md + .agentflow/test_strategy.md + contract stub commit. test_strategy.md captures coverage thresholds, integration scope, mock boundaries, and compliance-driven test requirements. Uses model from config (oracle field).

unit: checklist returns unresolved items when NFRs missing
checklist returns all resolved when all NFR fields present
artifact_generator produces valid tasks.json against schema
artifact_generator raises if two tasks share owned files
artifact_generator raises if any task would exceed file size ceiling
conversation loop appends assistant and user turns to history

tasks.acceptance_criteria: checklist resolves correctly; tasks.json passes agentflow validate; contracts committed to main

tasks.title: Enforce no-re-read constraint in worker prompt
tasks.description: Update agentflow/prompts/worker/v1/system.md and agentflow/worker/context_builder.py to explicitly instruct the worker agent not to use the Read tool on files already included in its context bundle. The context bundle already contains the full contents of all files in the task's reads list — re-reading them via tool call pays the token cost again without benefit. Add a prominent rule to the system prompt: 'Do not use the Read tool on any file listed in your Dependencies section — its contents are already in this context. Using Read on these files wastes tokens.' Also add an assertion in context_builder that logs a warning if bundle size exceeds 50k tokens, as a signal that the reads list may be too broad.

unit: system.md contains no-re-read rule
context_builder emits warning metric when bundle exceeds 50k tokens
no-re-read rule appears before the task description in the prompt order

tasks.acceptance_criteria: worker system prompt contains explicit no-re-read rule; bundle size warning emitted when threshold exceeded
